_comment: Proposed title corrections (spelling/spacing/capitalisation only) awaiting committee approval. Style: sentence case; named equipment designs and brands keep capitals; generic parts lowercase; 'cheekpiece' one word. Applied 2026-07-10 after committee approval.
s1-002: Hanging Cheek with cheekpiece attached to the mouthpiece and a Loose Ring
s2-019: Pinchless snaffle (central joint must have rounded edges)
s2-022: Mouthpiece with holes (bits must have a smooth surface)
s2-023: Flexible Mullen straight bar with lip guards
s2-026: Weymouth (largest and smallest ports illustrated; refer to the measuring bits section below)
s2-028: Curb bits with a ported/tilted mouthpiece
s2-037: Bit tape to cover the mouthpiece (latex or rubber)
s2-038: Bit tape to create a bit guard
s3-003: PS of Sweden noseband (with throat lash)
s3-004: PS of Sweden Nirak (with throat lash)
s3-005: PS of Sweden noseband (with throat lash)
s3-006: PS of Sweden High Jump bridle
s3-007: PS of Sweden Jump Off bridle
s3-009: Bridles with D-rings at the poll
s3-011: Noseband with metal sliding system attached to cheekpiece
s3-024: Blinkers of any type, including wool padding on cheekpiece
s3-029: Reins with hand stoppers or knotted reins
s4-001: Jackets: any solid colour is permitted. Wide contrast stripes and multi-coloured jackets and coats are not permitted (examples shown)
s4-007: Embossing/decoration at the top of the top boot only is permitted, but must be in the same colour scheme as the boot. The same applies to the cantle of the saddle.
s4-008: Boots can be of smooth or smooth-embossed surface. A neat and discreet zip on the outside area of the boot/chap is permitted.
s4-009: Coloured piping is permitted if it matches the piping on the riding jacket and/or saddle
s5-002: Jumping saddles and English side saddles
s5-009: Other examples of permitted safety stirrups
s6-001: Ear bonnets/hoods, but must not be attached to the noseband (no additional insulation and no ear plugs allowed)
s6-002: Tight-fitting head cover or titanium hoods are not permitted when horses are ridden. They are permitted in the stables/yards and when tied to horse floats.
s6-010: Running martingales are allowed in warm-up only on a snaffle bridle, but rein stoppers must be used
s7-001: Boots or bandages that cover the hoof
s7-004: Nailed or glued shoes must be clear if covering more of the hoof wall than a nailed shoe. Bulbs of the heel and full circumference of the coronary band must be visible.
s8-001: Spurs with a disc rowel in the vertical or horizontal plane (must be smooth and blunt)
s8-002: Rowel spurs with serrated tines, blunt or otherwise, are not permitted
s8-004: Smart Clix spurs with no serrated tines
s8-004b: Smart Clix spurs with serrated tines
s8-006: Coloured spurs (unless they match the rider's boots)
s8-011: Spursuader spurs / spurs with flat disc

_flags.s1-011: 'Shaped sleeved' left unchanged — meaning unclear in source; committee to advise (perhaps 'Shaped sleeve'?)
_flags.s4-006: 'Full length laced' -> 'Full-length laced boots' adds the implied word 'boots'
_flags.s5-001: Stray leading asterisk removed — check it wasn't a footnote marker in the annex
_flags.s3-005: Becomes identical to s3-003's title (they are different photos of the same product)
_flags.s3-029: 'or knotted' -> 'or knotted reins' completes the phrase
_flags.s7-004: Light grammar smoothing ('if covers' -> 'if covering'; 'than that of a nailed shoe' -> 'than a nailed shoe')
